key_insight: The paper gives black-box algorithms to recognize Suzuki groups Sz(q), construct standard generators, and perform constructive membership testing with no false positives.
engineering_relevance: This provides practical group-theoretic tooling for computer algebra systems, enabling reliable recognition and certification of elements in finite simple groups.
method: One-sided Monte Carlo algorithm for constructing standard generators of Sz(q).
Constructive membership test that expresses elements as a straight-line program in the standard generators.
Efficiently verifiable group presentation for Sz(q).
evidence_signal: The abstract reports algorithmic correctness properties and implementation in Magma, but does not give benchmark data or experimental results.
watch_item: As a Monte Carlo method, the algorithm may require randomized trials and its runtime/robustness can depend on the black-box group representation.
paper_title: Black Box Recognition of the Suzuki groups
